name: sign-guard
description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit|Bash) sign-gate for governance-SSOT files, implemented in hexa-lang (`_sign_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). A small registry (GATED) of high-stakes files ŌĆö currently `commons.tape` (cross-project do/dont layer), `project.tape` (per-project identity + governance), and `.gitignore` (commit-hygiene: silently controls what enters version control, so a stealth agent edit could un-ignore a secret or hide a file from review), and `~/.sidecar/local-paths` (the LOCAL-EXECUTION WHITELIST: a registered path-prefix silently overrides pool-route to force commands LOCAL, so a stealth add would route arbitrary commands off the pool) ŌĆö may be edited by the agent ONLY while a fresh user sign-off token exists. The user mints one with `sidecar sign <key>` (`commons` ┬Ę `project` ┬Ę `gitignore` ┬Ę `paths`), which writes the current epoch to `~/.sidecar/signs/<key>.sign`; a token is valid for 300s (5 min). Covers both write channels (mirrors hexa-native): structured file tools (Write/Edit/NotebookEdit) and the Bash redirect channel (`> X`, `>> X`, heredoc, `tee X`, `dd of=X`, `cp`/`mv` dest; relative targets resolved against `cwd`). Writes buried inside an invoked CLI (e.g. `sidecar init`'s own redirect) are not visible at the top-level command string, so the official scaffolder is unaffected ŌĆö only the agent's own direct writes gate. The hook CODE-ENFORCES the no-self-mint rule: it hard-denies the agent's mint command `sidecar sign <key>` (list / clear stay allowed) AND any write forging a token under `~/.sidecar/signs/`. Because the agent's tool calls (Bash ┬Ę Write/Edit) route through this hook, the agent CANNOT self-mint. Mint detection (0.1.5) is narrowed to a real command INVOCATION: the Bash channel is scanned on a QUOTE-MASKED copy of the command and `sidecar` must sit at a command position (first token or right after a shell separator `;`┬Ę`&&`┬Ę`||`┬Ę`|`┬Ę`&`), so a `sidecar sign <key>` MENTION inside a quoted commit message (`git commit -m 'ŌĆ”'`) or PR body (`gh pr --body 'ŌĆ”'`) is no longer mis-flagged as a self-mint ŌĆö pre-0.1.5 it was, wrongly blocking legitimate commits that merely referenced the command. The `bin/sidecar` /dev/tty layer remains the backstop against deliberate quote-evasion. (The interactive `!` bang a USER types in the TUI prompt does NOT route through the hook and mints freely ŌĆö the intended user path, safe because the agent cannot emit a `!` bang.) `bin/sidecar` carries an OPT-IN /dev/tty second layer (`SIDECAR_SIGN_REQUIRE_TTY=1`) that defeats hook-parser-evading obfuscation. The fresh token is the sole precondition (project.tape @D s11: gate on a real precondition, never an opt-out switch); enforcement is the hard deny (project.tape @D s13). REACTIVE ff-only stale-base auto-sync (0.2.0): before ALLOWING a signed edit to a gated SSOT file, `safe_ff_sync(<file>)` TRANSPARENTLY fast-forwards JUST that file to origin/<default> (`git checkout origin/<default> -- <file>`, FETCH_HEAD-gated fetch first) so the edit lands on a FRESH base ŌĆö the 2026-05-30 stale-base accident (a 28-commit-behind project.tape edit that would have REVERTED origin governance) can't start. SAFETY RAILS: ff-only (NEVER merge-commit/rebase/force) ┬Ę clean-path-only ┬Ę skip-on-doubt (no origin ┬Ę detached ┬Ę mid-rebase/merge ┬Ę untracked ┬Ę already-identical) ┬Ę FETCH_HEAD-gated fetch (>5min). NEVER blocks the edit: synced/skipped are SILENT, and on a dirty/diverged path it prints EXACTLY ONE advisory line (base diverged ŌåÆ re-check) and STILL allows the edit. To gate another file: add one `["key", "/suffix"]` row to GATED and reship. NO env-var / flag override by design.
